A significant crackdown has led to over 800 arrests and preventive detentions, targeting potential troublemakers in sensitive zones.

description: A man in Bengaluru was hospitalized after allegedly finding a lizard in his paneer tikka roll from a local bakery. He experienced vomiting and diarrhea, leading to a 24-hour observation period. Police have registered a case, though the complainant could not provide photographic evidence of the alleged contamination.

description: A 34-year-old software engineer, Puja Dutta, was found dead in her Bengaluru home under suspicious circumstances. Police suspect suicide due to the door being locked from the inside and cut marks on her body, though investigations are ongoing. Her parents have been contacted and are en route to the city.

title: Riyan Parag captured vaping inside dressing room during IPL match
description: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was filmed vaping in the team's dressing room during a live broadcast, sparking controversy. The act is illegal in India under the Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act, 2019, raising concerns about athlete health and regulatory compliance. This incident follows a previous breach of protocol by the RR manager.

title: Ghaziabad family’s ‘destination wedding’ at Almora cremation ground sparks massive row
description: A riverside cremation site in Uttarakhand's Almora district was used for a destination wedding, sparking outrage and prompting an administration probe. The ceremony, which involved a couple from Ghaziabad and around 50 guests, took place on April 25 at a ghat traditionally used for last rites.

'Politics has its place': Akhilesh Yadav visits BJP MLA who was injured while burning his effigy
description: SP leader Akhilesh Yadav visited BJP MLA Anupama Jaiswal in a Lucknow hospital after she sustained severe facial burns while attempting to set fire to an effigy. Yadav expressed his wishes for her speedy recovery, emphasizing that political differences should not overshadow compassion and human relationships. Jaiswal's husband lauded the visit as an act of "human sensitivity and democratic courtesy."

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
description: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.
